subject: Mathematics
description: determine the coordinates of a point that is a given fractional distance less than one from one end of a line segment to the other in one- and two-dimensional coordinate systems, including finding the midpoint

subject: Mathematics
description: derive and use the distance formula to verify geometric relationships, including congruence of segments and parallelism or perpendicularity of pairs of lines

subject: Mathematics
description: describe and perform transformations of figures in a plane using coordinate notation

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the image or pre-image of a given two-dimensional figure under a composition of rigid transformations, a composition of non-rigid transformations, and a composition of both, including dilations where the center can be any point in the plane

subject: Mathematics
description: identify the sequence of transformations that will carry a given pre-image onto an image on and off the coordinate plane

subject: Mathematics
description: distinguish between undefined terms, definitions, postulates, conjectures, and theorems

subject: Mathematics
description: investigate patterns to make conjectures about geometric relationships, including angles formed by parallel lines cut by a transversal, criteria required for triangle congruence, special segments of triangles, diagonals of quadrilaterals, interior and exterior angles of polygons, and special segments and angles of circles

subject: Mathematics
description: construct congruent segments, congruent angles, a segment bisector, an angle bisector, perpendicular lines, the perpendicular bisector of a line segment, and a line parallel to a given line through a point not on the line using a compass and a straightedge

subject: Mathematics
description: verify theorems about angles formed by the intersection of lines and line segments, including vertical angles and angles formed by parallel lines cut by a transversal, and prove equidistance between the endpoints of a segment and points on its perpendicular bisector

subject: Mathematics
description: prove two triangles are congruent by applying the Side-Angle-Side, Angle-Side-Angle, Side-Side-Side, Angle-Angle-Side, and Hypotenuse-Leg congruence conditions

subject: Mathematics
description: apply the definition of congruence, in terms of rigid transformations, to identify congruent figures and their corresponding sides and angles

subject: Mathematics
description: apply the definition of similarity in terms of a dilation to identify similar figures and their proportional sides and the congruent corresponding angles

subject: Mathematics
description: apply the Angle-Angle criterion to verify similar triangles and apply the proportionality of the corresponding sides to solve problems

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the lengths of sides and measures of angles in a right triangle by applying the trigonometric ratios sine, cosine, and tangent to solve problems

subject: Mathematics
description: apply the relationships in special right triangles 30-60-90 and 45-45-90 and the Pythagorean theorem, including Pythagorean triples, to solve problems

subject: Mathematics
description: identify the shapes of two-dimensional cross-sections of prisms, pyramids, cylinders, cones, and spheres and identify three-dimensional objects generated by rotations of two-dimensional shapes

subject: Mathematics
description: determine and describe how changes in the linear dimensions of a shape affect its perimeter, area, surface area, or volume, including proportional and non-proportional dimensional change

subject: Mathematics
description: apply the formula for the area of regular polygons to solve problems using appropriate units of measure

subject: Mathematics
description: determine the area of composite two-dimensional figures comprised of a combination of triangles, parallelograms, trapezoids, kites, regular polygons, or sectors of circles to solve problems using appropriate units of measure

subject: Mathematics
description: apply theorems about circles, including relationships among angles, radii, chords, tangents, and secants, to solve non-trivial problems
